definition: someone who is charming has very good manners and always behaves in a way that makes other people like them
example: he’s a very charming man
he had great personal charm

definition: criticism is a statement that expresses disapproval of someone or of something they have done. Criticism is also the activity of making statements like this
example: I got plenty of criticism for my first exhibition
he refused to openly criticise the government
the new theatre was criticised for being ugly
she’s always making critical comments about my piano playing
he spoke critically of the company’s failure to recruit good staff

definition: someone who is diplomatic is always careful about what they say, making sure that they do not offend anyone
example: she’s always very diplomatic
he answered all her questions diplomatically, if not truthfully

definition: someone who is direct always says exactly what they think, even if it might upset or offend people
example: she’s always very direct and says what she means

definition: if you go off somewhere, you leave where you are and go somewhere else for a while
example: I could go off and have a coffee and she’d still be talking when I got back

definition: if someone is intense, they are very serious in the way they talk and behave
example: she’s very focused, very intense
she looked at me with real intensity

definition: someone who is loyal supports someone or something reliably and without changing
example: he’s very loyal — always there for you when you need him
despite the setbacks, they loyally supported him
the servants showed great loyalty even when times were difficult
after 20 years with the company, it felt disloyal to leave and work for a competitor

definition: someone who is modest does not like to talk about their successes or abilities
example: he’s very successful, but also incredibly modest
“I was just one of the team,” he said modestly
he described the achievement with great modesty

definition: if children are naughty, they behave badly and do things they are not supposed to do
example: his children are really naughty

definition: if you take something personally that someone has said to you, you are offended by it because you feel they are criticising you and not anyone else. If you criticise someone personally, you deliberately say something critical about them
example: he gets upset easily and takes things very personally
it’s nothing personal – I just meant you need to practise more

definition: if you push yourself, you make yourself work hard because you want to achieve something important or significant. If you push someone else to do something, you make them do it by encouraging them very firmly or forcing them to do it
example: they really push pupils to achieve high marks
he’s clever enough, but lazy. He needs a bit of a push

definition: a scholarship is money that is given to someone by an organisation so that they can afford to go to school or university
example: he won a scholarship to study physics at Cambridge

definition: someone who is sensitive is easily affected by what other people say about them and gets offended very easily
example: he’s very sensitive and gets upset very easily
you need to consider her sensitivities more

definition: if someone is showing off, they are trying to impress people by talking about their achievements or possessions in a very annoying way
example: he never shows off or boasts about anything
he was a show-off and worse, a liar

definition: someone who is shy feels nervous when they are with other people and finds it difficult to talk, especially with people they do not know
example: she’s very quiet and shy
the brothers looked shyly at their mother’s friend

definition: if someone is sweet, they are pleasant, kind, and nice to other people
example: he’s very sweet and affectionate
she smiled sweetly and sat down

definition: if someone is active in a role or organisation, they take part and do things rather than just saying they support it
example: we can play an active role in helping with childcare
he is actively involved in his local church

definition: childcare is all the activity involved in looking after children. Childcare also refers to the arrangements that working people make for someone to look after their children while they are at work
example: we can play an active role in helping with childcare

definition: complications are problems that make a situation or task more difficult to deal with
example: one further complication was that my passport had expired

definition: if something is true contrary to what people think, it really is true although most people do not realise it and think the situation is different
example: contrary to common belief, the average age of becoming a grandparent is 51

definition: if you contribute to something, you provide something useful either by what you say or what you do
example: grandchildren contribute to the relationship by helping their grandparents stay up-to-date
his book was a valuable contribution to modern literature

definition: if you estimate something, you try to work out its approximate amount, value, or strength
example: experts estimate it will take 1,000 tons of rock to fill the hole
at a rough estimate, it will cost between two and three thousand pounds
a rough estimation of the Sun’s temperature
an estimated 300 soldiers were killed or wounded

definition: if you have fond memories of someone or something, it always gives you pleasure when you think about them or remember them
example: I have fond memories of my grandmother
I’ll always fondly remember those summers spent in Ireland
she remembers her grandparents with fondness

definition: if you interfere, you become involved in a situation when your help or advice is not wanted
example: grandparents should not interfere with their children’s parenting
we cannot accept any interference in our internal procedures
an interfering neighbour
translation: назойливый, вмешивающийся не в свои дела

definition: leisure is time when people are not working and can do activities that they enjoy
example: we’ll have to spend less on leisure and travel

definition: a notion is an idea or belief or opinion about something
example: my notion of the role of grandparents is problematic

definition: if something poses some kind of danger or problem, it causes it
example: bare wires pose a risk of electric shock

definition: you use potentially to say that something might happen or become possible in the future
example: we can potentially play a more active role in helping with childcare
he has the potential to become a great golfer
he has the potential to become a great golfer

definition: if you are reluctant to do something, you do not really want to do it
example: I was reluctant to give up my job
he came with us rather reluctantly
I couldn’t understand his reluctance to go on the trip

definition: your role is the purpose or position that you have in a particular situation
example: we can potentially play a more active role in helping with childcare

definition: if someone rules in a situation, their influence and power is greater than anyone else’s, and they control what happens
example: a lack of discipline can produce spoilt children who rule the house

definition: if you make a sacrifice, you give something up in order to be able to do or have something else instead
example: being president demands a lot of sacrifice
we need to increase productivity without sacrificing quality

definition: if parents spoil a child, they always allow the child to have whatever he or she wants, which can have a bad effect on the way the child behaves and develops
example: my grandmother would spoil us the whole time we were there
she acted like a spoiled child, doing whatever she wanted

definition: someone’s status is the role or position that they have in society, as seen by other people
example: I’m not yet comfortable with my new status as a grandmother

definition: a stereotype is something that people think is typical of a particular person, place, or thing, especially when this belief is unfair or untrue
example: the stereotype of a granny is an old lady with white hair and glasses
she spoke with a stereotypical Liverpool accent
women are stereotypically more emotional than men
she spoke with a stereotypical Liverpool accent

definition: if you turn a particular age, you become that old
example: I only recently turned 50

definition: something that is typical is normal and has all the same features as most of the other things of the same sort
example: she looked like my idea of a typical granny
typically, each exam is taken by about 2,000 students

definition: something that is vague is not clear or definite
example: I had only vague memories of my own grandparents
I can vaguely remember my first day at school

definition: if you apologise, you say that you are sorry for something bad or wrong that have done or said
example: we’re both very stubborn and I didn’t want to be the first to apologise
please accept my sincere apology for not replying earlier
he was very apologetic and said he would never drink again
the waiter apologetically explained that they had no more steak

definition: an awkward situation is one in which people feel slightly embarrassed
example: it was quite awkward meeting her again after all that time
we shook hands a little awkwardly, then left

definition: if you bump into someone you know, you meet them unexpectedly somewhere because you are both there at the same time
example: a few years later, I bumped into her at a conference

definition: if you are dating someone, you see them regularly because you are starting a romantic relationship with them
example: we dated for two years before we got engaged
they went to the cinema on their first date

definition: if things are drifting, no one is controlling events or planning what should happen. If two people drift apart, they gradually become less friendly or their relationship gradually ends because neither of them does anything to keep the friendship alive
example: people drift apart because they don’t have enough in common

definition: if you fall out with someone, you have an argument with them and you both stop talking to each other
example: look – I don’t want to fall out with you about this
we had a big falling-out a few years ago

definition: if you are glad about something, you are happy about it. If you are glad to do something, you are very willing to do it
example: I’m glad we split up
our support team will gladly help you

definition: if you hang out with someone, you spend a lot of your free time with them. If you hang out somewhere, you spend a lot of your free time there
example: we used to hang out together all the time

definition: if you say that someone is an idiot, you mean they are very foolish or stupid
example: our boss was a bit of an idiot
that was an idiotic thing to do

definition: if you are in touch with someone, you still see them and talk to them sometimes. If you lose touch, you stop seeing them or talking to them, especially when they move to a different town
example: we still keep in touch via Facebook

definition: if two or more people meet up, they go somewhere they have arranged to go to in order to do something together socially
example: let’s meet up in the main square at 7 tonight

definition: a move is something that you do as part of an attempt to achieve something
example: she made the first move in their relationship

definition: someone who is outgoing is friendly and lively, and likes meeting new people
example: Sandra remembers him as a very outgoing person

definition: someone’s personality is their character and how they usually appear to other people
example: she has a very outgoing personality

definition: if you regret something you have done, you feel bad about it and wish you hadn’t done it
example: I deeply regret what happened that day in Rome
he has finally expressed regret for his unacceptable behaviour

definition: to remain in a particular state or condition means to stay that way without changing
example: we’ve remained friends ever since primary school

definition: someone who is stubborn does not like to change their mind about anything and does not listen to people who try to persuade them to change
example: we’re both very stubborn and I didn’t want to be the first to apologise
Patrick shook his head stubbornly
my father used to get so angry at her stubbornness via  /ˈvaɪə/ Preposition via something means by using that thing or by going through that place we kept in touch via email and social media

definition: if you wish something, you want it to be true although you know it is impossible
example: I wish I hadn’t left school when I was still so young

definition: someone or something that is adaptable can change when necessary in order to succeed at something
example: these plants are highly adaptable to most environments
we need to adapt to living with climate change
plants have evolved many adaptations to cope with fire

definition: your ancestors are all the members of your family who lived before you, your grandparents, their parents, and so on into the past
example: my ancestors came over with William the Conqueror in 1066
I’m English, but I have some Spanish ancestry through my great-grandfather

definition: on a knife, sword, axe etc the blade is the flat part with a sharp edge
example: make sure you keep the blade sharp and clean

definition: cultivation is the preparation of land and the growing of plants on it, usually for food
example: cultivation of crops has been going on here for three thousand years
rice and other crops are cultivated in the valleys

definition: to reveal something means to make it known, especially when it was something that was previously unknown or secret
example: this newspaper can exclusively reveal the date of the next election
the revelation came as a surprise to his friends, and even to his family

definition: a sample of something is a small piece of it which can be used to provide information about the whole thing
example: he was sent a sample issue of the magazine

definition: if you trace the beginning and development of something, you find out how it started and what happened as it developed through time
example: the project aims to trace the route taken by our ancestors from Africa
